mission.title: litellm: [Bug]: LiteLLM 1.82.1 OpenRouter Model IDs no longer working
mission.description: [Bug]: LiteLLM 1.82.1 OpenRouter Model IDs no longer working. This issue affects 18+ users.

steps.title: Identify litellm troubleshoot symptoms
steps.description:
Check for the issue in your litellm deployment:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n litellm -l app.kubernetes.io/name=litellm
kubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=litellm -n litellm --tail=100 | grep -i error
```
Look for errors or warnings in the logs that may indicate the issue.

steps.title: Review litellm configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant litellm configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all -n litellm -l app.kubernetes.io/name=litellm
kubectl get configmap -n litellm -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=litellm
```
### Check for existing issues

steps.title: Apply the fix for [Bug]: LiteLLM 1.82.1 OpenRouter Model IDs no longer working
steps.description:
We hit the same issue after upgrading to 1.82.3. All OpenRouter models started returning `400 "not a valid model ID"` — the proxy was sending `openrouter/google/gemini-3-pro-preview` instead of `google/gemini-3-pro-preview`.

Root cause is the early-return in `get_llm_provider()` added by #22320
```yaml
Error fetching response:Error: 400 litellm.BadRequestError: OpenrouterException - {"error":{"message":"openrouter/google/gemini-3-flash-preview is not a valid model ID","code":400},"user_id":"user_2s3Tb9aAYUh7JZG8C0uQRqpHIts"}. Received Model Group=openrouter/google/gemini-3-flash-preview
Available Model Group Fallbacks=None
```

steps.title: Confirm [Bug]: LiteLLM 1.82.1 OpenRouter Model IDs no… is resolved
steps.description:
Verify the fix by checking that the original error no longer occurs:
```bash
kubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=litellm -n litellm --tail=50 --since=5m
kubectl get events -n litellm --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm that the issue symptoms are gone.

resolution.summary:
We hit the same issue after upgrading to 1.82.3. All OpenRouter models started returning `400 "not a valid model ID"` — the proxy was sending `openrouter/google/gemini-3-pro-preview` instead of `google/gemini-3-pro-preview`.

Root cause is the early-return in `get_llm_provider()` added by #22320 (commit `09ef5e67`).
codeSnippets:
Error fetching response:Error: 400 litellm.BadRequestError: OpenrouterException - {"error":{"message":"openrouter/google/gemini-3-flash-preview is not a valid model ID","code":400},"user_id":"user_2s3Tb9aAYUh7JZG8C0uQRqpHIts"}. Received Model Group=openrouter/google/gemini-3-flash-preview
Available Model Group Fallbacks=None
OpenrouterException - {"error":{"message":"openrouter/nvidia/nemotron-3-super-120b-a12b:free is not a valid model ID","code":400},"user_id":"user_xxxx"}.

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with litellm installed or the issue environment reproducible.
